# LiPy: Odia Handwritten Character Recognition

> LiPy is an academic project that uses deep learning (EfficientNetB0) to recognize handwritten Odia characters. It includes an OCR web application, a crowdsourced dataset platform (LiPyD), and a FastAPI inference backend.

## Project Info

- **Repository:** https://github.com/biranchikulesika/lipy
- **Website:** https://lipy.app (or local development at http://localhost:3000)
- **License:** MIT
- **Stack:** Next.js, FastAPI, TensorFlow/Keras, Supabase, Hugging Face Hub, Azure, Vercel
- **Dataset:** 2,002+ crowdsourced handwritten samples across 41 Odia character classes (34 consonants + 7 vowels)
- **Model:** EfficientNetB0 trained on 64×64 grayscale images via transfer learning

## Pages

- [Home (OCR Workspace)](/): Draw, upload, or capture an Odia character for handwriting recognition.
- [LiPyD Dataset](/lipyd): Contribute handwritten Odia character samples to the open dataset.
- [About](/about): Project overview, system architecture, data pipeline, model training, deployment, and roadmap.
- [Team](/team): The five-member development team from Utkal University and NIELIT Bhubaneswar.

## Architecture

- **Frontend:** Next.js + Tailwind CSS deployed on Vercel
- **Backend:** FastAPI inference service deployed on Azure
- **Model:** EfficientNetB0 exported via TensorFlow/Keras, hosted on Hugging Face Hub
- **Dataset:** Stored on Hugging Face Datasets (biranchikulesika/lipy)
- **Contributor Data:** Supabase (PostgreSQL + Storage) for LiPyD samples and admin auth
- **Training Notebook:** Google Colab (L.ipynb) with complete end-to-end pipeline

## Key Technical Details

- Image input is preprocessed to 64×64 grayscale → RGB, normalized [0,1]
- Inference returns status (success / low_confidence / ambiguous) with confidence scores
- Prediction threshold: LOW_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD = 0.60, AMBIGUOUS_MARGIN = 0.10
- Top-K predictions: 3
- Label format: CONS_KA, VOW_A, etc. mapped to Odia Unicode characters

## Dataset Structure

- Complete dataset: ~2,002 images across 41 classes
- Minimum samples per class: 25
- File naming: `<CATEGORY>_<CHARACTER>_<UUID>.png` (e.g., `CONS_KA_abc123.png`)
- Categories: Consonants (CONS_) and Vowels (VOW_)
- Supported formats: PNG, JPG/JPEG
- Data augmentation during training: rotation ±15°, shift ±10%, shear ±10%, zoom ±10%, brightness 0.9–1.1
